en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is trading near 66.9, slightly above its 20-period simple moving average at 66.47, indicating a modest bullish bias in the short-term trend. The latest session range between 66.23 and 66.97 shows steady buying interest without excessive volatility. Price action holding above the moving average suggests buyers remain in control, although the advance is currently gradual rather than impulsive. A sustained move above recent highs would strengthen the bullish structure.

Momentum indicators provide a cautiously constructive outlook. The MACD value remains positive at 0.3809, which typically reflects upward momentum and supports the case for continued gains. However, the absence of a signal line and RSI reading limits confirmation of trend strength. As a result, traders should monitor whether momentum continues to build alongside price appreciation. If MACD remains positive while price consolidates above the moving average, the market could be preparing for another leg higher.

From a technical perspective, immediate support is located around the SMA zone near 66.47, followed by stronger support around 66.20–66.25, which aligns with the recent session low. Resistance is visible near 67.00, where price recently stalled, with a secondary resistance area around 67.50 if buyers achieve a breakout. A decisive close above 67.00 could attract additional momentum traders, while failure to hold above support may trigger short-term profit-taking.

For trading purposes, bullish participants may consider buying on pullbacks toward the 66.40–66.50 region, provided price remains above support and momentum stays positive. More aggressive traders could look for breakout entries above 67.00 with confirmation from rising volume. Risk management remains essential, with protective stops below 66.20 to limit downside exposure. Conversely, if the market falls below support and closes beneath the moving average, a deeper correction toward lower levels would become increasingly likely.
